description: Inflammation of the appendix. Pain typically starts around the navel and moves to the lower right abdomen. A medical emergency if untreated.
recommended_action: Go to emergency room immediately. Surgical removal (appendectomy) may be required.

description: A chronic condition affecting the large intestine. Symptoms are managed long-term. No structural damage occurs.
recommended_action: Dietary changes, stress management, and fiber intake. Consult a gastroenterologist for persistent symptoms.

symptoms: pain improves with eating or antacids
description: Open sores that develop on the inner lining of the stomach or small intestine. Often caused by H. pylori bacteria or NSAIDs.
recommended_action: Avoid NSAIDs and alcohol. Seek diagnosis for H. pylori. Take prescribed antacids or proton pump inhibitors.

symptoms: bright red blood on toilet paper or in bowl
description: Swollen veins in the rectum or anus. The most common cause of rectal bleeding. Usually not serious.
recommended_action: Increase fiber intake, sitz baths, topical creams. Consult doctor if bleeding persists or is heavy.

symptoms: change in bowel habits lasting weeks
description: Cancer of the colon or rectum. Early detection via colonoscopy significantly improves prognosis.
recommended_action: Immediate medical evaluation required. Colonoscopy and imaging needed for diagnosis.

symptoms: pain radiating to left arm or jaw
description: Occurs when blood flow to a part of the heart is blocked. A life-threatening emergency requiring immediate medical intervention.
red_flags: Any chest pain with arm/jaw radiation
chest pain with shortness of breath
recommended_action: Call emergency services (112/911) immediately. Do not drive yourself to the hospital. Chew aspirin if available and not allergic.

symptoms: pain worsens when lying down or after eating
description: Chronic acid reflux causing stomach acid to flow back into the esophagus, irritating its lining.
red_flags: Chest pain that spreads to arm/jaw
recommended_action: Avoid trigger foods, eat smaller meals, elevate head while sleeping. Use antacids or PPIs as prescribed.

symptoms: sudden sharp chest pain (worse on breathing in)
description: A blood clot blocking one of the pulmonary arteries in the lungs. Life-threatening and requires urgent care.
recommended_action: Emergency room immediately. This is life-threatening. Anticoagulant therapy required.

symptoms: infrequent bowel movements (fewer than 3 per week)
description: A common condition where bowel movements are infrequent or difficult. Often due to low fiber diet or dehydration.
recommended_action: Increase fiber and water intake. Regular exercise. Use stool softeners if needed. Consult doctor if persistent.

symptoms: productive cough with yellow or green mucus
description: Infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
recommended_action: See a doctor immediately. May require antibiotics, chest X-ray, and hospitalization in severe cases.

description: Chronic inflammatory disease of the airway that causes episodic breathing difficulties.
red_flags: unable to speak in full sentences
recommended_action: Use rescue inhaler immediately. Avoid triggers. If no relief, seek emergency care (status asthmaticus).

description: A severe bacterial infection caused by Vibrio cholerae. A medical emergency due to severe dehydration.
recommended_action: Emergency rehydration therapy (ORS or IV fluids). Immediate hospitalization required.

symptoms: progressive difficulty swallowing solids then liquids
description: Cancer of the esophagus. Often diagnosed late. Progressive swallowing difficulty is the hallmark symptom.
recommended_action: Urgent referral for endoscopy and biopsy. Oncology consultation needed.

symptoms: spinning sensation (vertigo) triggered by head position changes
description: The most common cause of vertigo. Occurs when calcium crystals in the inner ear become displaced.
recommended_action: Epley maneuver performed by a physiotherapist or ENT. Avoid sudden head movements.

symptoms: sudden numbness or weakness on one side
description: Occurs when blood supply to the brain is interrupted. Brain cells begin dying within minutes. Use FAST acronym (Face, Arms, Speech, Time).
recommended_action: Call emergency services (112/911) immediately. Time is brain - every minute matters for clot-busting treatment.

symptom_topic: Eye discomfort and redness in adults
description: A sudden increase in intraocular pressure that can cause permanent vision loss within hours if untreated.
red_flags: Sudden severe eye pain with vision loss
recommended_action: Ophthalmology emergency. Immediate intervention needed to reduce eye pressure and prevent blindness.

symptom_topic: Eye discomfort and redness in adults
symptoms: redness in one or both eyes
description: Inflammation of the conjunctiva. Can be viral (most common), bacterial, or allergic.
red_flags: blurry vision that doesn't clear with blinking
recommended_action: Viral: self-limiting, clean discharge, avoid contact. Bacterial: antibiotic eye drops. Allergic: antihistamine drops.

symptoms: usually on one side of head
description: A neurological disorder causing recurrent, often debilitating headaches that interfere with daily life.
red_flags: Sudden worst headache of your life (thunderclap)
headache with fever and stiff neck
recommended_action: Rest in a dark quiet room. Triptans or prescribed migraine medication. Identify and avoid triggers.

symptoms: sudden thunderclap headache (worst headache of life)
description: Bleeding in the space surrounding the brain. Often caused by a ruptured aneurysm. Life-threatening emergency.
recommended_action: Emergency 112/911 immediately. CT scan and neurosurgical intervention required.

description: An irregular and often rapid heart rate that can increase the risk of stroke, heart failure, and other heart-related complications.
recommended_action: Seek immediate medical evaluation. ECG needed. May require rate control medications or cardioversion.

symptoms: inability to walk or bear weight
description: A serious injury common in older adults with osteoporosis. Requires urgent surgical intervention.
red_flags: severe hip pain after any injury in elderly
recommended_action: Emergency care immediately. X-ray required. Surgical repair (ORIF or hip replacement) typically needed.

symptoms: gradual hip pain worsening over time
description: Degenerative joint disease causing progressive cartilage breakdown in the hip joint.
recommended_action: Weight management, physical therapy, NSAIDs for pain. Joint replacement if severe.

description: A tear of one of the major ligaments in the knee, often from sports pivoting or landing. Common in athletes.
red_flags: severe swelling within hours of injury
recommended_action: RICE (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation). Orthopedic evaluation. MRI for confirmation. Surgery may be required.

symptoms: numbness or tingling in leg or foot
pain worsens with sitting or bending
description: A displaced disc in the lumbar spine pressing on spinal nerves. A common cause of sciatica.
red_flags: Loss of bladder or bowel control
recommended_action: Rest, NSAIDs, physiotherapy. MRI for confirmation. Surgery (microdiscectomy) for severe neurological compromise.

symptoms: numbness or tingling in saddle area (groin inner thighs)
loss of bladder or bowel control
description: Compression of the cauda equina nerve bundle. A surgical emergency if not treated within hours.
red_flags: Bladder or bowel incontinence with back pain
recommended_action: Emergency surgical decompression required immediately to prevent permanent paralysis.

description: Inflammation of the sinuses. Acute sinusitis (under 4 weeks) is usually viral. Chronic sinusitis persists over 12 weeks.
recommended_action: Saline nasal rinse, decongestants, steam inhalation. Antibiotics for bacterial sinusitis confirmed by doctor.

description: A life-threatening complication of diabetes where the body produces excess ketones. Most common in Type 1 diabetics.
recommended_action: Emergency hospitalization immediately. IV fluids, insulin, and electrolyte replacement required.

description: Age-related wear-and-tear of the cervical spine. Very common in adults over 40.
red_flags: loss of bladder or bowel control
recommended_action: Physiotherapy, neck exercises, NSAIDs. Collar support. Surgery for myelopathy or severe cord compression.

symptom_topic: Numbness or tingling in hands in adults
symptoms: numbness in thumb, index, and middle fingers
tingling in hands especially at night
description: Compression of the median nerve as it passes through the carpal tunnel in the wrist.
recommended_action: Wrist splints at night, avoid repetitive wrist flexion. Corticosteroid injection. Surgery (carpal tunnel release) for severe cases.

description: A pregnancy that develops outside the uterus (usually in a fallopian tube). Life-threatening if the tube ruptures.
red_flags: Positive pregnancy test with pelvic pain
recommended_action: Emergency room immediately. Ultrasound required. Surgical or medical management (methotrexate) essential.

description: Tissue similar to the uterine lining grows outside the uterus, causing inflammation and scar tissue.
red_flags: Pelvic pain so severe it prevents daily activity
pain with intercourse that is new or worsening
recommended_action: Hormonal therapy, pain management, laparoscopic surgery for diagnosis and treatment.

description: Twisting of the spermatic cord, cutting off blood supply to the testicle. A surgical emergency — testis may be lost within 6 hours.
recommended_action: Emergency surgery (detorsion) required IMMEDIATELY within 6 hours to save the testicle.

description: Fluid accumulation in the lungs. Often caused by heart failure. A life-threatening emergency.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately. Sit upright. Oxygen therapy. Diuretics and cardiac support in hospital.

symptoms: pain and weakness in the shoulder
description: A tear in one or more of the four rotator cuff tendons of the shoulder. Common in people over 40 and athletes.
recommended_action: Rest, ice, NSAIDs. Physiotherapy. MRI for diagnosis. Surgery for complete tears or persistent pain.

symptoms: severe sore throat with sudden onset
red and swollen tonsils with white patches
description: Bacterial throat infection caused by group A Streptococcus. Needs antibiotic treatment to prevent rheumatic fever.
recommended_action: Throat swab for culture. Antibiotics (penicillin or amoxicillin) for confirmed strep. Avoid school/work for 24 hours.

description: Inflammation of the epiglottis. A life-threatening emergency that can rapidly obstruct the airway.
recommended_action: Emergency 112/911 immediately. Do not attempt to examine throat. Airway management is priority.

description: Bacterial infection in the urinary system. More common in women. Usually involves the bladder (cystitis).
red_flags: Fever with back or flank pain (kidney involvement)
recommended_action: Increase water intake. Antibiotics prescribed after urine culture. Avoid holding urine.

description: A chronic inflammatory lung disease causing obstructed airflow. Primarily caused by smoking. Progressive.
recommended_action: Stop smoking immediately. Bronchodilators and corticosteroids. Pulmonary rehabilitation. Oxygen therapy for severe cases.

symptom_topic: Foot pain or ankle pain in adults
symptoms: stabbing heel pain especially on first steps in the morning
pain at the bottom of heel
description: Inflammation of the plantar fascia — the thick band of tissue connecting the heel bone to the toes.
recommended_action: Stretching exercises, supportive footwear, NSAIDs, orthotics. Corticosteroid injection or extracorporeal shock wave therapy.

symptom_topic: Foot swelling or leg swelling in adults
symptoms: pain or tenderness in leg (especially calf)
warmth and redness over the swollen area
description: A blood clot in a deep vein, usually in the leg. Can be life-threatening if the clot moves to the lungs (pulmonary embolism).
red_flags: sudden chest pain or shortness of breath (PE)
recommended_action: Urgent Doppler ultrasound confirmation. Anticoagulant therapy (heparin, warfarin, or DOAC) required.

description: Inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord, typically caused by a viral infection.
red_flags: Rapidly worsening headache and stiff neck
recommended_action: Immediate emergency department visit. Lumbar puncture (spinal tap) for diagnosis. Antiviral therapy.

symptoms: periods of normal behavior in between attacks
description: A medical emergency where part of the intestine folds into another section. Most common in infants 6 months to 3 years.
red_flags: colicky pain with vomiting in infant
recommended_action: Emergency room immediately. Air or contrast enema for reduction. Surgery if unsuccessful.

symptoms: fewer than 3 bowel movements per week
description: Very common in children, often related to dietary issues, toilet training problems, or avoidance after a painful stool.
recommended_action: Increase fluid and fiber intake. Toilet routine. Laxatives (polyethylene glycol) as prescribed. Exclude Hirschsprung's if severe.

description: A viral infection causing swelling around the larynx and trachea. Most common in children aged 6 months to 3 years.
recommended_action: Cool mist or steam humidifier. Corticosteroids (dexamethasone). Nebulized epinephrine for moderate-severe cases.

symptoms: dehydration signs (dry mouth, no tears, sunken eyes, decreased urination)
description: The leading cause of severe diarrheal disease in children under 5 worldwide. Highly contagious.
red_flags: less than 1 wet diaper in 8 hours
recommended_action: Oral rehydration solution (ORS) is critical. IV fluids if severely dehydrated. Zinc supplementation for children over 6 months.

symptoms: ear pain (child may pull or tug at ear)
description: Infection of the middle ear. The most common childhood infection requiring antibiotic treatment.
red_flags: Redness and swelling behind the ear (mastoiditis)
recommended_action: Pain relief (paracetamol/ibuprofen). Antibiotics (amoxicillin) for children under 2 or severe cases. Watchful waiting in mild cases over 2.

symptoms: seizure or convulsion triggered by high temperature
description: Convulsions triggered by fever in children typically between 6 months and 5 years of age. Usually benign.
red_flags: child does not return to normal after seizure
recommended_action: Lay child on side (recovery position). Call emergency services. Do not restrain. Anti-seizure medication if prolonged.

symptoms: non-blanching petechial rash (spots that don't fade under glass)
description: Life-threatening bacterial infection of the blood and/or membranes surrounding the brain. Rash is a late, ominous sign.
recommended_action: Emergency services (112/911) immediately. IV antibiotics (benzylpenicillin) must be given immediately. This is a true medical emergency.

symptoms: moderate to severe head pain (often bilateral in children)
description: Migraine is common in children. Attacks are usually shorter than in adults and may present differently.
red_flags: headache with fever and neck stiffness
recommended_action: Rest in dark room, paracetamol or ibuprofen. Identify triggers. Triptan nasal spray for older children with severe migraine.

symptom_topic: Joint pain or muscle pain in children
symptoms: joint swelling and pain lasting more than 6 weeks
description: The most common type of childhood arthritis. An autoimmune condition causing chronic joint inflammation.
red_flags: Joint pain with eye redness or pain (uveitis)
systemic fever and rash with joint swelling
recommended_action: NSAIDs for pain. Disease-modifying drugs (methotrexate, biologics). Regular eye checks for uveitis.

symptom_topic: Joint pain or muscle pain in children
symptoms: aching pain in both legs (thighs, calves, or behind knees)
occurs in the evening or nighttime
description: Common benign limb pain in children aged 3-12. Etiology is unclear but is non-serious.
recommended_action: Parental reassurance, massage, stretching, and heat pad. No specific treatment needed.

symptoms: itchy blistering rash (starts on trunk then spreads)
rash progresses from red spots to fluid-filled blisters then crust
description: A highly contagious viral illness caused by varicella-zoster virus. Vaccine-preventable.
recommended_action: Antiviral (aciclovir) for high-risk children. Calamine lotion, antihistamines for itch. Isolate from school.

symptoms: painful sores and blisters in mouth
flat red spots or blisters on hands and feet
description: A common viral infection in children under 5 caused by Coxsackievirus. Usually mild and self-limiting.
recommended_action: Supportive care only. Paracetamol for fever/pain. Encourage fluids. Isolate from school or daycare.

symptoms: white or yellow coating on tonsils
description: Inflammation of the tonsils, most commonly due to viral or bacterial (streptococcal) infection.
red_flags: muffled voice with trismus (quinsy peritonsillar abscess)
recommended_action: Pain relief, warm fluids. Antibiotics for confirmed bacterial tonsillitis. ENT referral for recurrent cases (tonsillectomy).

description: UTI in children, especially infants, can indicate underlying structural renal issues. Requires prompt treatment.
red_flags: Fever in infant under 3 months with suspected UTI
recommended_action: Urine culture before antibiotics. Oral antibiotics for simple UTI. IV antibiotics for pyelonephritis. Renal ultrasound for recurrent cases.

description: Inflammation of the small airways (bronchioles), most commonly caused by RSV (Respiratory Syncytial Virus). Most common in infants under 2.
recommended_action: Supportive care: nasal suctioning, ensure hydration. Oxygen for low saturation. Hospitalization for severe cases.

symptom_topic: Eye discomfort and redness in children
symptoms: redness and swelling around the eye
description: Infection of the soft tissues of the eye socket behind the orbital septum. A life-threatening complication can cause vision loss or spread to brain.
recommended_action: Emergency hospitalization with IV antibiotics. CT scan to exclude abscess requiring surgical drainage.
specialist: Pediatric Ophthalmologist / Pediatric ENT / Emergency Medicine

symptoms: nasal congestion lasting more than 10 days
thick yellow or green nasal discharge
description: Bacterial sinusitis in children often follows a viral URI. Symptoms persist longer than in simple colds.
recommended_action: Saline nasal rinse. Antibiotics (amoxicillin-clavulanate) for bacterial sinusitis. Decongestants for symptom relief with physician guidance.

symptoms: projectile non-bilious vomiting in infant aged 2-8 weeks
description: Narrowing of the pylorus (stomach outlet) causing obstruction in infants. Requires surgical treatment.
red_flags: Projectile vomiting in infant under 2 months
recommended_action: Ultrasound for confirmation. Correction of electrolytes. Pyloromyotomy (surgery) is curative.

symptoms: redness and swelling behind the ear (mastoid area)
description: Bacterial infection of the mastoid bone behind the ear. A complication of untreated otitis media. Can spread to the brain.
red_flags: redness and swelling behind the ear
recent ear infection with high fever
recommended_action: Urgent ENT referral. IV antibiotics. CT scan. Surgical drainage (mastoidectomy) if abscess present.

description: A rare but serious eye tumor in children, most common under 5 years. Detected early with strong visual prognosis.
red_flags: White glow in the pupil in photos
recommended_action: Immediate ophthalmology referral. Eyes under anesthesia examination (EUA). Oncology involvement.
